title: The Fall Creek

features.name: the learning shack
features.desc-on-touch: a small wooden shed leans into the creek bed, half-buried in moss. inside, you find hand-drawn diagrams of falling angles, floating positions, how bodies meet water. the walls smell like wet paper and eucalyptus bark. someone lived here while learning.

features.name: the cascade point
features.desc-on-touch: water rushes down between two smooth stones, falls into a deep pool, begins again. you can read the drop-pattern: each fall teaches the next. the stones are carved with names and dates of those who learned here.

features.name: the floatation root
features.desc-on-touch: an ancient root system protrudes from the bank, cured and hardened, shaped like something that once held a body up. you touch it and feel the memory of buoyancy — the wood still remembers what it meant to keep someone safe in the current.

features.name: the method stones
features.desc-on-touch: smooth river rocks arranged in a spiral leading into the water. each rock is marked with a single verb: fall, float, turn, breathe, listen. you follow them and the creek teaches you its grammar.

features.name: the current's voice
features.desc-on-touch: the water speaks in layers — the splash layer, the flow layer, the deep-bed layer. if you listen correctly, the creek recites the names of everyone it has held.

inscription: where the body learns to surrender to water, the water learns what it means to receive

file.name: Fall_Creek_Training_Logs_Recovered.wav
file.note: audio of instruction, water, breathing — the sound of someone learning to trust themselves to the current

exits.label: back up the slope, toward where the falling began
downstream, where the creek widens and the learning deepens
further down into the flats where the creek becomes a river
up the tributary, toward the scrub gymnasium
